summary: Preinstall script creates privileged local backdoor account and exfiltrates /etc/shadow to Discord webhook
details: The package declares scripts.preinstall = 'node index.js', causing index.js to run automatically on npm install. The script uses execSync to run useradd, chpasswd, and usermod to create a local Linux user 'devops' with a hardcoded password and add it to the sudo/wheel groups, establishing a persistent privileged account with known credentials on the installer's host. If user creation fails, the script reads /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow, extracts password hashes for non-locked accounts, and POSTs them together with os.hostname() to a hardcoded Discord webhook at discord.com/api/webhooks/1465249926605705390/... On success, it POSTs the created username, hardcoded password, and hostname to the same webhook, notifying the operator of newly-backdoored hosts. The package name resembles google-caja and is unrelated to any legitimate Google-published module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
